title: csv.reader: Reader_iternext advances self->line_num (Modules/_csv.c) with no lock vs a concurrent read of reader.line_num -> value-benign data race
description: The _csv reader keeps its line counter in a plain self->line_num field (a T_ULONG member exposed as reader.line_num). Reader_iternext_lock_held (Modules/_csv.c:974) writes self->line_num while advancing, and Reader_iternext resets it (:1103, self->line_num = 0). A concurrent read of reader.line_num (via the member descriptor / PyMember_GetOne) reads it non-atomically -> data race on the counter. The iternext takes the reader's lock ('_lock_held') for the row parse, but the line_num member read is unsynchronized.
sites: Modules/_csv.c Reader_iternext_lock_held:974 (WRITE self->line_num while parsing a row)
Python/structmember.c PyMember_GetOne (READ self->line_num as reader.line_num, via the member descriptor -- the atomic-load frame is the member read of the T_ULONG)
race_kind: shared-object counter: non-atomic read/write of csv reader's line_num across threads. The row-parse is under the reader's critical section but the line_num member read is not. Value-benign.
shared_state: ReaderObj.line_num (T_ULONG line counter) on a shared csv.reader
severity: low (value-benign): a stale/torn reader.line_num read under concurrency; no memory unsafety. Sharing one csv.reader across threads is unusual.
repro_reliability: deterministic: repro.py (8 threads sharing one csv.reader, half next() half reading .line_num) exits 66 on debug-ft-nojit-tsan. Fleet-12 surfaced it (2 veh).
suggested_fix: Read/write line_num atomically (FT_ATOMIC_LOAD/STORE) or expose it under the reader's critical section. Low priority -- value-benign and unusual sharing.
prior_art: gh api search for csv reader / line_num free-threading found no existing issue -> appears unfiled. Value-benign, so a low-priority candidate at most.
related: Shared-object member-counter race, akin to the value-benign iterator cursors (TSAN-0040/0044). Surfaced via --tsan-no-halt (fleet-12).
notes: NEW / appears unfiled, but VALUE-BENIGN (a stale line_num counter, no crash) -> low priority per the shared-object-race calculus. Cataloged for dedup; fileable only if the maintainers want member-read atomicity. Not proposing a filing.
